title: Iranian press/newspaper periodical counts and literacy rates, 1898-1988 — extracted from Encyclopaedia Iranica 'COMMUNICATIONS in Persia'
source_org: Encyclopaedia Iranica (Encyclopaedia Iranica Foundation), article by Annabelle Sreberny-Mohammadi and Ali Mohammadi
frequency: one-off reference article with embedded snapshot statistics
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica — academic reference, cite author/article per their citation guidance
notes: This CSV is a topic-scoped extract of a single Encyclopaedia Iranica article ('COMMUNICATIONS in Persia') whose FULL text is stored (not duplicated) at data/raw/iran-telecom-history/iranica-communications-in-persia/communications-in-persia.txt -- see SOURCE_NOTE.txt in this folder and the sibling manifest.json for full retrieval method/citation/failure details. Key finding for the media-history hunt: Iran's press count actually CONTRACTED after 1979 -- 237 periodicals (1979-80, pre-university-closure) down to 163 by 1988, versus the Pahlavi-era peak framing (198 papers in 1976) -- a genuine 'before and after' data point for the post-1979 media economy shift. Also captures the 1976 literacy-rate gender gap (57% male / 32% female, age 10+) as a companion figure to the White-Revolution-era illiteracy data already in data/raw/iran-white-revolution-corps/. All numbers transcribed exactly as printed in the source; nothing fabricated.
